title: show unified output details
description: in the unified outputs list box on the upper-half of the screen, split it in half. in the lefthand side should be the existing list of displays+highlight; the new right side box will contain details of the output. If it's configured or detected by niri, we will display all of those details in the box; Truncate cleanly if output details overflow the box, otherwise space them out evenly

title: data structure/state-machine to show TUI section/entry focus state
description: There should be an added state machine, perhaps represented as an Enum data structure, that is continuously updated by the input cycle of the TUI application. This input state-machine would then be referenced in all rendering components to show highlight state for a child element or to show/hide self, and so on.

comments.author: Worker rikimaru
comments.text: working on this with human-written code, so far; main loop needs work

comments.author: Worker rikimaru
comments.text: look at KanshigTuiState as the organizing state machine for the application; it should reflect where the focus and highlights in the app are; this drives how things are displayed in the application itself

comments.author: Worker rikimaru
comments.text: impl of TUI state complete. some kinks to work out, but it's done

title: Add popup help screen tied to ?
description: Add a global keybinding, similar to 'q to quit', to open a popup in the center of the screen that lists all of the current controls. it should be written so that more help commands/explanations can be added easily in the future. The ? key should make a the popup appear, and pressing any key afterward will make the popup disapear. For this ticket, the help popup should show all of the currently bound input keys and what they are tied to. Repeats of the same action for different keys should be grouped together (e.g. j+s+down for 'down')

title: Show profile details
description: in the lower panel, profiles, split that box in half and show details of the selected profile (listed on left side) in the righthand side. Details should be the names of each display in the profile, and their enable/disable status. If one of the displays listed in the profile is ALSO in the niri-outputs (ie currently active and detected), the details should show this for that display in any profile in which it appears

title: validate kanshi config
description: with a loaded kanshi config file a String, validate that its entries are well-formed 'profile' or 'output' sections, as shown in READMD.md . Write tests with example file content, similar to what's in the README.md, that validates that profiles have matching delimeters and content only expected parameters with value content.
close_reason: Completed: Validation logic implemented with comprehensive tests

title: Create kanshig crate and CLI parsing per design
description: Read the README.md file and then the KANSHIG-PLAN.md file. Following the instructions in the file, DO ONLY: create a new rust workspace and workspace sub-crate named 'kanshig-cli'. It be a cargo --bin project. It can be located at kanshig-cli/ within the root of the repository. It should have idiomatic rust cli flag parsing code for the flag specified in the plan document, the -c / --config flag. The program should be able to read and load the kanshi config file from the filesystem based on whether a flag was provided. It does not need to do anything else at this time
close_reason: Completed: Created kanshig-cli crate with CLI argument parsing for -c/--config flag

title: Create CHANGES.md, bring up to date
description: Review the README.md, KANSHIG-PLAN.md and git history and produce a bulleted list, with newest entries on top, with dates and commit hash(es), summarizing changes to the kanshig codebase. add instructions in AGENTS.md to instruct agents to add new entries at the top of the CHANGES.md file as part of the 'pre-br-bead-close' workflow
close_reason: Completed: CHANGES.md created with git history and AGENTS.md updated

title: Add TUI Frame, mouse support
description: Review the tui.rs module, bootstrap and display the frames implemented in this module in main.rs; create a TUI frame and also enable mouse support. Focus on concise code that follows the lifecycle of the application
close_reason: Completed: TUI application implemented with ratatui, mouse support enabled, and frames from tui.rs integrated

title: command line flag to return JSON of unified outputs
description: add a command-line flag that, when invoked, runs the process to ONLY get unified outputs (so: read kanshi config and get outputs & read niri msg outputs) and returns the unified outputs list and all details as JSON. the normal TUI should be skipped and the program should terminate after returning the JSON

title: Load validated kanshi config into data model structs
description: once we have tests and implementation to validate whether a config has valid data, we need code to load that data into the corresponding rust data model structs. This should also have tests, compared known or expected in the text format with what is loaded into data model structs.

title: BUG: strange outputs highlight behavior + can't select detected-only outputs
description: Currently, in the upper box that shows the unified list of outputs, if an entry is detected+configured (i.e. it appears in both output lists), it can't be selected/highlighted in the TUI. There is also strange behavior with the state of the output box when switching BACK from the profiles box. It is based, in the human's setup with three outputs (one detected+configured) and three profiles, on weird behavior when jumping back and forth. Also as mentioned above the third entry for the detected + configured display can't be highlighted UNLESS one switches back from the third choice in the profiles window. Something is wonky with the KanshigTuiState variants and how they swap data back and forth. The bug is probably there in that input handling + output counter selecting code

title: call niri msg outputs json output and capture
description: Use Rust's std::process::Command system to invoke calls out to the system shell. In this ticket, we want to call 'niri msg --json outputs' (note the --json flag, this is needed for you to read the output as structured data) and capture what the command returns in STDOUT. It should be parsed, via serde_json, into instances of the data model niri outputs data model. Check the README.md file for examples of the niri msg --json outputs JSON format. Write tests for this as well.

title: Combine Outputs and Niri Outputs screen
description: the last TUI block displayed, Niri Outputs, should have its contents rolled into the first TUI block, Outputs. Each line, which presently corresponds to an output, should have potential labels that say 'DETECTED' or 'CONFIGURED' in different, bright colors. It would be possible to have both labels. You have the 'DETECTED' label if your output entry appears in the Niri Outputs screen/data model. You may ALSO or INSTEAD have  The label 'CONFIGURED' . The labels should appear before the rest of the text in the TUI describing the output entry. This also means that the two data structures should be merged. Or perhaps there should be a single structure for both entry that includes the checkbox. If there are duplicates, by some metric, then they should merge into a single outputs entry with both flags checked to 'true'. Please ask any questions if you need more guidance. If I, the SXC give you more guidance, append it to this description.
close_reason: required manual intervention from human; got task completed

comments.author: Worker rikimaru
comments.text: qwen3 failed to do this correctly twice; currently both niri-outputs and kanshi config outputs are dupe-listed in box; no input control yet

title: command line flag to reload kanshi configs
description: add a command-line flag that, when invoked, invokes a process to make the kanshi systemd daemon restart (and hence reload configs); should be idempotent/safe to reinvoke etc

title: load kanshi config from fs as string content and display
description: with a located kanshi config file with greater than zero content length, use Rust's std::fs module to load the file as a string, then display it in STDOUT via log

title: add TUI display to list all profiles and outputs in the kanshi config
description: with kanshi config data loaded into model structs, we want to have a simple, proof-of-concept display of those items on the screen. It should be two separate tables that list outputs-from-config and profiles-from-config. They should be two separate boxes, at 100% horizontal width, with a cursor to show each of the entries selected. It should be possible to move from table to table with the tab key to cycle focus

title: Create detailed plan document for kansig utility
description: Read the README.md for this repository; Based on the description of  outlined there, create a detailed plan for how to implement a Rust Text User Interface application, using the ratatui create, to perform the functionality described in the README.md . Be as detailed as necessary to guide a future programmer, maybe you, on what the application should contain functionally, as well as in terms of architectural code design, dependant crates/libraries and any other similar concerns.
close_reason: Completed: Created detailed implementation plan in kansig-plan.md
